notes: This local contract is independent from tw-rpc v1 and Relay v1/v2.
controlTargetId, controlEpoch, leaseId and fence are terminal-control namespace values.
Input adapters MUST NOT query status and then write a backend themselves; input operations execute inside the authority critical section.

ping advertises additive capabilities. A missing capabilities field is a valid legacy response and means output.rendered-snapshot, activity.agent-status, and activity.agent-result are unsupported; Feishu must preflight required support before relying on these observations.
input.scroll is the lease-fenced semantic tmux history path; controlled adapters MUST NOT send terminal mouse escape bytes directly to a pane.

Feishu is the only exclusive owner class. Dashboard, Relay, controlled CLI, and tw-serve producers share one interactive lease and fence; an interactive release detaches only that producer, never fences remaining interactive writers, and returns the target to FREE when the last registered producer releases cleanly.

Leases are liveness-bounded and must be renewed. An idle expired/restarted non-Feishu HELD lease is fenced, exact-target checked, output-capture reset, and safely abandoned to FREE; Feishu, DRAINING, and accepted/in-doubt operations remain RECOVERY_REQUIRED.
output.tail is a read-only Feishu marker-correlation capability, not visible PTY output or tmux scrollback. Its controlEpoch and outputGeneration fence late markers without granting input ownership.

output.rendered-snapshot is an additive read-only capability for the exact Feishu lease while HELD or DRAINING. It returns a generation- and pane-fenced bounded tmux rendering; consumers must never fall back to raw output as a reply payload.

activity.agent-status is an additive read-only capability for the exact Feishu lease while HELD or DRAINING. A running observation includes an opaque provider/session/turn boundary for the exact Claude or Codex structured transcript; terminal text is never a result source.

activity.agent-result is an additive read-only capability for the exact Feishu lease while HELD or DRAINING. It requires the persisted activity source, re-proves the target generation and stopped pane before and after extraction, and returns only the correlated final assistant message from the Agent-owned structured transcript. Missing durability is retryable; ambiguity, source mismatch, malformed transcripts, or unsupported providers fail closed.
Agent final responses return at most 16 KiB of UTF-8 text from the beginning of the answer and report truncation explicitly.
Rendered snapshots return at most 128 KiB of UTF-8 tail data and report truncation. A bounded capture failure does not by itself change target lifecycle or input ownership.

Within one outputGeneration, the capture uses two bounded 4 MiB segments and exposes a monotonically increasing absolute byte cursor while retaining approximately the newest 4-8 MiB. A cursor older than the retained base returns STALE_OUTPUT_CURSOR without changing target lifecycle or input ownership.

A Feishu marker consumer may resume a retention-stale cursor from the guaranteed 4 MiB retained window only after reproving the same lease, fence, controlEpoch, and outputGeneration; it must rebuild parser state without replaying input. Any authority change remains fail closed.

Old outputGeneration artifacts are garbage-collected only after the generation fence. A legacy output-only OUTPUT_CONTINUITY_UNCERTAIN state follows the existing idle non-Feishu repair rule: the exact backend must be current, ownership FREE, no operation or handoff uncertain, and the previous owner must not be Feishu; every other continuity uncertainty remains fail closed.
handoff.commit is the durable transfer point and requires a persisted drain disposition.
